body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 68/2026
Made under: Environmental Management Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 68/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under theENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT ACT[section 108.2] andPARK ACT[section 29]. Order in Council 161/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with
         the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective October 1, 2026,
         the Conservation Officer Service Authority Regulation, B.C. Reg. 318/2004, and the
         Park, Conservancy and Recreation Area Regulation, B.C. Reg. 180/90, are amended as
         set out in the attached Schedule. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; N. SHARMA,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Conservation Officer Service Authority Regulation 1	Section 1 (2) of the Conservation Officer Service Authority Regulation, B.C.
         Reg. 318/2004, is amended by adding the following paragraph: (a.31)Firearm Violence Prevention Act;. Park, Conservancy and Recreation Area Regulation 2	Section 56 (c) of the Park, Conservancy and Recreation Area Regulation, B.C.
         Reg. 180/90, is repealed. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC conservation officers can enforce Firearm Violence Prevention Act starting October 1, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has updated two regulations to give conservation officers the authority to enforce the new Firearm Violence Prevention Act. The Conservation Officer Service Authority Regulation now lists the Firearm Violence Prevention Act as a law officers can enforce. Also, a section of the Park, Conservancy and Recreation Area Regulation that previously limited enforcement powers has been removed. These changes take effect on October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Conservation officer firearm enforcement authority takes effect
